summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting focused on tornado recovery updates, personnel actions for the fire department, and the purchase of property to expand the government campus. The board approved several new part-time firefighter hires, accepted donations for tornado recovery, discussed long-term recovery committee efforts affecting 298 damaged structures in Goshen Township, and authorized the purchase of property at 6765 Goshen Road for $125,000 to expand the township government complex. A temporary firehouse plan using a mobile home at the old police station site was also discussed.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for August 3-16, 2022
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Brad Taylor effective 8/5/22
Hired part-time firefighter EMT-B Colin Westman at $15.50/hour effective 8/28/22
Hired part-time firefighter EMT-B Scott Harley at $15.50/hour effective 8/28/22
Hired part-time firefighter paramedic (West) at $17/hour effective 8/28/22
Approved out-of-town travel for Administrator Steve Pegram to ICMA conference in Columbus not to exceed $800
Accepted $200 donation from Dillon Amstead for tornado recovery efforts
Accepted $2,032.23 donation from Goshen Lions Club for tornado recovery efforts
Appropriated $127,000 in ARP line item 2272-760-710-1000 for land purchase
Approved 3% pay raise for Zoning Administrative Assistant Alyssa Hudley retroactive to August 14, 2022
Approved Resolution R-047-2022 authorizing purchase of real property at 6765 Goshen Road
Approved purchase of property at 6765 Goshen Road from Van Landingham Brothers Inc. for $125,000

notable_topics: Tornado recovery updates and long-term recovery committee progress
Damage assessment: 451 structures affected, 298 in Goshen Township
State aid threshold not met (16 of 25 needed heavily damaged/uninsured properties)
Property purchase at 6765 Goshen Road for government campus expansion
Temporary firehouse setup using mobile home at old police station
Fire department staffing: one resignation and three new hires
Radio tower repair timeline (4-5 weeks to power up)

attendees: Dan (Trustee Allen, referenced in donation discussion)
Aaron Ellerman (Police Chairman, Long-Term Recovery Committee Chairman)
Captain Hampton (credited with mobile home plan)
notes: Trustee Hausman is also referred to as Trustee Hoffman at various points in the transcript, likely the same person. Items K and L were tabled mid-meeting pending executive session discussion about funding, then approved afterward. Executive session ran from 7:31 PM to 8:41 PM covering complaints about a public official, employee compensation, and property purchase. The Moscow tornado and Dayton 2019 tornado were referenced as benchmarks for long-term recovery timelines. Insurance is covering the mobile home/temporary firehouse costs with the condition they recoup costs when no longer needed. The old antenna on the building will be removed due to damage and being out of service.
